name: Gary Howard Kidgell
background: 13th-generation American from San Jose, CA. Building consultant. Family roots in California dating back to 1849 with ancestors involved in Silicon Valley development and military service. Author of memoir 'The Forefathers' Son' and a 110-page 'GHK Day One Plan'. Has engaged on policy issues at state and national levels since at least 2007.
statement: Legacy builder delivering affordable solutions for everyday Californians'

positions.economy: No new wealth taxes; fiscal discipline and budget management; oppose policies that discourage innovation; public-private partnerships
positions.housing: Stabilize rents and expand supply; workforce housing acceleration; vacant commercial conversion; first-time homebuyer tools; cost-decline pass-through requirements
positions.affordability: California Affordability Roadmap across 6 categories: (1) Food — mandatory pass-through of wholesale price drops, shrinkflation disclosure, margin transparency; (2) Utilities — Middle-Class Energy Rebates, microgrid/rooftop solar support; (3) Transportation — tax credits, fuel price transparency, affordable EV access, auto insurance fairness; (4) Housing — rent stabilization, workforce housing; (5) Insurance — Affordability Review Board, transparent claims handling; (6) Enforcement — Affordability Enforcement Division with whistleblower rewards
positions.government: Legacy Leadership philosophy; public-private partnerships; transparency and civic investment over new taxes
